data: Epitaph and relief for Kissos and Tryphon
First observed outside the 'porta sud', in the former Garden of the 'Compagnia di Gesu'
Photo from IGPalermo tav.XXI, by permission of the Museo Archeologico Regionale Antonino Salinas
Partially reopened following extensive restoration work. Comprehensive collection of archaeological material, predominantly from the West of the island, with an important Etruscan collection.
Centre of Palermo, Piazza Olivella 24, 90100 - Palermo

epitaph and relief for kissos and tryphon
ISic001120. Epitaph and relief for Kissos and Tryphon

Unknown
1Κίσσος καὶ Τρύφων οἱ Εἰκαδίου
2[ταλαίπωρ]οι καὶ ἄωροι χρηστο[ὶ]
3χαίρετε

Text encoding: Dmitry Dundua, Robert Crellin
Κίσσος καὶ Τρύφων οἱ Εἰκαδίου ταλαίπωροι καὶ ἄωροι χρηστοὶ χαίρετε

Unknown
1ΚΙΣΣΟΣΚΑΙΤΡΥΦΩΝΟΙΕΙΚΑΔΙΟΥ
2[........]ΟΙΚΑΙΑΩΡΟΙΧΡΗΣΤΟ[.]
3ΧΑΙΡΕΤΕ

Text encoding: Dmitry Dundua, Robert Crellin
ΚΙΣΣΟΣΚΑΙΤΡΥΦΩΝΟΙΕΙΚΑΔΙΟΥΤΑΛΑΙΠΩΡΟΙΚΑΙΑΩΡΟΙΧΡΗΣΤΟΙΧΑΙΡΕΤΕ

Facsimile Images

Apparatus criticus

  • Text of IGPalermo no.33

English translation

Jonathan Prag

Kissos and Tryphon, sons of Eikadios, miserable and untimely (in death), good men, farewell!

commentary

Muscolino argues that the piece is probably an import in the ?early 16th century? from the Cyclades, since all the parallels for such a piece come from that region. The importation must have been early, since the piece was already recorded by Antonio Agustin in the mid 16th century in Palermo.

Muscolino argues that the piece is probably an import in the ?early 16th century? from the Cyclades, since all the parallels for such a piece come from that region. The importation must have been early, since the piece was already recorded by Antonio Agustin in the mid 16th century in Palermo.
